action_items.task: Call the Community Action Agency to set up an intake for utility assistance
action_items.context: To prevent electricity shut-off due to a $280 balance.

action_items.task: Provide shut-off notice and a paystub to the Community Action Agency
action_items.context: Required documentation to process the LIHEAP grant.

action_items.task: Contact PO if the Community Action Agency cannot assist with the utility bill
action_items.context: To explore alternative charity options before electricity is disconnected.

action_items.task: Continue taking Lisinopril daily without skipping doses
action_items.context: To maintain healthy blood pressure levels required for the DOT physical.

action_items.task: Complete DOT physical at the clinic
action_items.context: Mandatory requirement before beginning trucking academy classes.

action_items.task: Provide the DOT medical card to the PO
action_items.context: Proof of passing the DOT physical.

action_items.task: Begin classes at Tri-State Trucking Academy
action_items.context: Enrolled for Monday-Thursday evening CDL training classes.

action_items.task: Comply with intrastate travel restrictions; do not cross state lines while driving
action_items.context: Standard condition of supervision regarding CDL employment.
Routine monthly check-in before the client's work shift.

critical_updates.details: Client maintains residence at County Road 9 but received a $280 electric shut-off notice scheduled for disconnection next Thursday. Applying for LIHEAP assistance to resolve the balance.
Client is working approximately 35 hours per week at the Co-op, handling feed and inventory. Experiencing financial strain due to a lack of available overtime.
Client was approved by WIOA for full tuition assistance. Successfully enrolled at Tri-State Trucking Academy for CDL training, with classes starting on the 28th.
High blood pressure is currently well-managed at 125/80 using daily Lisinopril medication, with no major side effects. A DOT physical is scheduled for next Tuesday.
Client remains compliant with supervision conditions. Reports zero law enforcement contact, tickets, or warnings.
Client reports remaining clean with no alcohol or drug use. No missed UAs on file.

next_episode_context: Client is stably housed but facing a $280 electric shut-off next Thursday; applying for LIHEAP via the Community Action Agency. Working ~35 hours/week at the Co-op. Approved for WIOA funding and starts CDL classes at Tri-State Trucking Academy on the 28th. Needs a DOT physical next Tuesday; blood pressure is currently managed well with Lisinopril. Fully compliant with supervision conditions. Next check-in is scheduled for the 12th at 9:00 AM.
